누가복음 4:1.content: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness

누가복음 4:2.content: for forty days, being tempted by the devil. He ate nothing in those days. Afterward, when they were completed, he was hungry.

누가복음 4:3.content: The devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread.”

누가복음 4:4.content: Jesus answered him, saying, “It is written, ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.’ ”

누가복음 4:5.content: The devil, leading him up on a high mountain, showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.

누가복음 4:6.content: The devil said to him, “I will give you all this authority, and their glory, for it has been delivered to me; and I give it to whomever I want.

누가복음 4:7.content: If you therefore will worship before me, it will all be yours.”

누가복음 4:8.content: Jesus answered him, “Get behind me Satan! For it is written, ‘You shall worship the Lord your God, and you shall serve him only.’ ”

누가복음 4:9.content: He led him to Jerusalem, and set him on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, cast yourself down from here,

누가복음 4:10.content: for it is written, ‘He will put his angels in charge of you, to guard you;’

누가복음 4:11.content: and, ‘On their hands they will bear you up, lest perhaps you dash your foot against a stone.’ ”

누가복음 4:12.content: Jesus answering, said to him, “It has been said, ‘You shall not tempt the Lord your God.’ ”

누가복음 4:13.content: When the devil had completed every temptation, he departed from him until another time.

누가복음 4:14.content: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee, and news about him spread through all the surrounding area.

누가복음 4:15.content: He taught in their synagogues, being glorified by all.

누가복음 4:16.content: He came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up. He entered, as was his custom, into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and stood up to read.

누가복음 4:17.content: The book of the prophet Isaiah was handed to him. He opened the book, and found the place where it was written,

누가복음 4:18.content: “The Spirit of the Lord is on me, because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent me to heal the broken hearted, to proclaim release to the captives, recovering of sight to the blind, to deliver those who are crushed,

누가복음 4:19.content: and to proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord.”

누가복음 4:20.content: He closed the book, gave it back to the attendant, and sat down. The eyes of all in the synagogue were fastened on him.

누가복음 4:21.content: He began to tell them, “Today, this Scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing.”

누가복음 4:22.content: All testified about him, and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth, and they said, “Isn’t this Joseph’s son?”

누가복음 4:23.content: He said to them, “Doubtless you will tell me this parable, ‘Physician, heal yourself! Whatever we have heard done at Capernaum, do also here in your hometown.’ ”

누가복음 4:24.content: He said, “Most certainly I tell you, no prophet is acceptable in his hometown.

누가복음 4:25.content: But truly I tell you, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the sky was shut up three years and six months, when a great famine came over all the land.

누가복음 4:26.content: Elijah was sent to none of them, except to Zarephath, in the land of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow.

누가복음 4:27.content: There were many lepers in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet, yet not one of them was cleansed, except Naaman, the Syrian.”

누가복음 4:28.content: They were all filled with wrath in the synagogue, as they heard these things.

누가복음 4:29.content: They rose up, threw him out of the city, and led him to the brow of the hill that their city was built on, that they might throw him off the cliff.

누가복음 4:30.content: But he, passing through the middle of them, went his way.

누가복음 4:31.content: He came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee. He was teaching them on the Sabbath day,

누가복음 4:32.content: and they were astonished at his teaching, for his word was with authority.

누가복음 4:33.content: In the synagogue there was a man who had a spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a loud voice,

누가복음 4:34.content: saying, “Ah! what have we to do with you, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are: the Holy One of God!”

누가복음 4:35.content: Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!” When the demon had thrown him down in the middle of them, he came out of him, having done him no harm.

누가복음 4:36.content: Amazement came on all, and they spoke together, one with another, saying, “What is this word? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out!”

누가복음 4:37.content: News about him went out into every place of the surrounding region.

누가복음 4:38.content: He rose up from the synagogue, and entered into Simon’s house. Simon’s mother-in-law was afflicted with a great fever, and they begged him for her.

누가복음 4:39.content: He stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her. Immediately she rose up and served them.

누가복음 4:40.content: When the sun was setting, all those who had any sick with various diseases brought them to him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.

누가복음 4:41.content: Demons also came out of many, crying out, and saying, “You are the Christ, the Son of God!” Rebuking them, he didn’t allow them to speak, because they knew that he was the Christ.

누가복음 4:42.content: When it was day, he departed and went into an uninhabited place, and the multitudes looked for him, and came to him, and held on to him, so that he wouldn’t go away from them.

누가복음 4:43.content: But he said to them, “I must preach the good news of God’s Kingdom to the other cities also. For this reason I have been sent.”

누가복음 4:44.content: He was preaching in the synagogues of Galilee.
